notes: Spec asserts the ready → working → ready arc with TotalTokens > 0 after the turn. Codex's headless `codex exec` is single-shot: the daemon sees proc-PID pre-session born ready, the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then ready → working on transcript activity (the assistant turn). `codex exec` then terminates, so the turn-end is observed as process_exited on the rollout session rather than a working → ready state flip — there is no interactive REPL to return to ready. The TotalTokens > 0 assertion isn't expressible in the phase DSL today (no metric form in the invariant DSL).

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
User prompt → working on the rollout session
Headless turn completes — codex exec exits; never enters waiting
